1	//! String table and interned-string operations — port of `lstring.c` + `lstring.h`.
2	//!
3	//! Provides two key abstractions:
4	//!
5	//! - [`LuaStringImpl`]: the Lua string value, stored as a reference-counted byte slice.
6	//!   Short strings (`<= MAX_SHORT_LEN` bytes) are interned in the process-global
7	//!   [`StringPool`]; long strings are heap-allocated on each creation and never
8	//!   interned.
9	//!
10	//! - [`StringPool`]: the intern table for short strings, stored on `GlobalState`.
11	//!   Replaces the C `stringtable` struct, which used an open-addressing hash table
12	//!   with intrusive chaining through `TString.u.hnext`.  In Rust the intrusive
13	//!   chain is dropped; a `HashMap` provides O(1) lookup and automatic rehashing.
14	//!   See PORT NOTE on [`StringPool`] for the full rationale.
15	//!
16	//! The `lstring.h` header is merged into this module per PORTING.md §1.
17	//!
18	//! # C source files

109	/// Whether a Lua string is short (interned) or long (not interned).

111	/// Corresponds to `LUA_VSHRSTR` / `LUA_VLNGSTR` tags from `lobject.h`.

116	/// LUA_VLNGSTR → LuaStringImpl::Long   (shrlen = 0xFF sentinel; u.lnglen holds length)
117	/// ```
118	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
119	pub enum StringKind {
120	    // C: LUA_VSHRSTR — shrlen byte holds the length; string is interned
121	    Short,
122	    // C: LUA_VLNGSTR — shrlen = 0xFF sentinel; u.lnglen holds the real length
123	    Long,
124	}

126	/// A Lua string: an immutable, reference-counted byte sequence.
127	///
128	/// Short strings (`<= MAX_SHORT_LEN = 40` bytes) are interned in the
129	/// [`StringPool`] on `GlobalState`; two short strings with the same bytes
130	/// are guaranteed to be the same `GcRef` (pointer equality via `Rc::ptr_eq`).
131	///
132	/// Long strings are heap-allocated independently and never interned.  Their
133	/// hash is computed lazily on first call to [`hash_long_str`] and cached via
134	/// interior mutability (`Cell<u32>`).
135	///
136	/// # C mapping (types.tsv)
137	/// ```text
138	/// TString             → LuaStringImpl
139	/// TString.extra       → extra: Cell<u8>   (reserved-word idx for Short; hash-ready flag for Long)
140	/// TString.shrlen      → kind: StringKind   (0xFF sentinel replaced by enum variant)
141	/// TString.hash        → hash: Cell<u32>
142	/// TString.u.lnglen    → bytes.len()        (length implicit in Rc<[u8]>)
143	/// TString.u.hnext     → (removed)          (intrusive chain gone; StringPool uses HashMap)
144	/// TString.contents    → bytes: Rc<[u8]>
145	/// ```
146	pub struct LuaStringImpl {
147	    // C: char contents[];  (flexible array member)
148	    bytes: Rc<[u8]>,
149	
150	    // C: lu_byte shrlen;  (0xFF for long strings, actual length for short)
151	    // Replaced by the StringKind enum; length is implicit in bytes.len().
152	    kind: StringKind,
153	
154	    // C: unsigned int hash;
155	    // Using Cell<u32> so that `hash_long_str` can cache the hash through a
156	    // shared `&LuaStringImpl` reference (interior mutability, single-threaded).
157	    hash: Cell<u32>,
158	
159	    // C: lu_byte extra;
160	    // Short strings: reserved-word token index (0 = not a keyword).
161	    // Long strings:  0 = hash not yet computed; 1 = hash is valid.
162	    extra: Cell<u8>,
163	}

226	impl PartialEq for LuaStringImpl {
227	    /// Equality for Lua strings.
228	    ///
229	    /// For short strings (interned), pointer equality via `Rc::ptr_eq` is sufficient
230	    /// and matches `eqshrstr` in C.  For long strings, we fall back to byte
231	    /// comparison, matching `luaS_eqlngstr` in C.
232	    fn eq(&self, other: &Self) -> bool {
233	        if self.kind == StringKind::Short && other.kind == StringKind::Short {
234	            // C: eqshrstr(a, b) — pointer equality; macros.tsv: Rc::ptr_eq(a, b)
235	            Rc::ptr_eq(&self.bytes, &other.bytes)
236	        } else {
237	            // C: luaS_eqlngstr — byte comparison for long strings
238	            self.bytes == other.bytes

343	/// Test equality of two long strings.
344	///
345	/// Two long strings are equal if they have identical byte content.  A pointer
346	/// equality short-circuit is also applied: if `a` and `b` share the same
347	/// underlying `Rc<[u8]>` allocation, they are trivially equal.
348	///
349	/// # C source
350	/// ```c
351	/// // C: int luaS_eqlngstr(TString *a, TString *b) {
352	/// //   size_t len = a->u.lnglen;
353	/// //   lua_assert(a->tt == LUA_VLNGSTR && b->tt == LUA_VLNGSTR);
354	/// //   return (a == b) ||
355	/// //     ((len == b->u.lnglen) &&
356	/// //      (memcmp(getlngstr(a), getlngstr(b), len) == 0));
357	/// // }
358	/// ```
359	pub(crate) fn eq_long_str(a: &LuaStringImpl, b: &LuaStringImpl) -> bool {
360	    // C: lua_assert(a->tt == LUA_VLNGSTR && b->tt == LUA_VLNGSTR);
361	    // macros.tsv: lua_assert → debug_assert!
362	    debug_assert!(a.is_long() && b.is_long(), "eq_long_str: both arguments must be long strings");
363	
364	    // C: (a == b) — pointer equality (same TString allocation)
365	    // In Rust: check if the Rc<[u8]> byte buffers are the same allocation
366	    if Rc::ptr_eq(&a.bytes, &b.bytes) {
367	        return true;
368	    }
369	
370	    // C: (len == b->u.lnglen) && (memcmp(getlngstr(a), getlngstr(b), len) == 0)
371	    // macros.tsv: getlngstr → ts.as_bytes()
372	    a.as_bytes() == b.as_bytes()
373	}

375	// C: unsigned int luaS_hash(const char *str, size_t l, unsigned int seed)
376	// lstring.h: LUAI_FUNC → pub(crate)
377	/// Hash a byte string with a seed using Lua's FNV-style hash.
378	///
379	/// This is a pure function with no allocations.  The algorithm XORs shifts and
380	/// additions over each byte in reverse order, seeded by `seed ^ len`.
381	///
382	/// # C source
383	/// ```c
384	/// // C: unsigned int luaS_hash(const char *str, size_t l, unsigned int seed) {
385	/// //   unsigned int h = seed ^ cast_uint(l);
386	/// //   for (; l > 0; l--)
387	/// //     h ^= ((h<<5) + (h>>2) + cast_byte(str[l - 1]));
388	/// //   return h;
389	/// // }
390	/// ```
391	///
392	/// PORT NOTE: C parenthesises `(h<<5)` and `(h>>2)` explicitly, so the outer
393	/// additions are unambiguous despite C's `<<`/`>>` having lower precedence than
394	/// `+`.  In Rust `<<` and `>>` have higher precedence than `+`, so the same
395	/// expression is computed without extra parentheses; `wrapping_add` is used to
396	/// match C's unsigned wrap-around arithmetic.
397	pub(crate) fn hash_bytes(bytes: &[u8], seed: u32) -> u32 {
398	    // C: unsigned int h = seed ^ cast_uint(l);
399	    // macros.tsv: cast_uint → x as u32
400	    let mut h: u32 = seed ^ (bytes.len() as u32);
401	
402	    // C: for (; l > 0; l--)
403	    let mut l = bytes.len();
404	    while l > 0 {
405	        l -= 1;
406	        // C: h ^= ((h<<5) + (h>>2) + cast_byte(str[l - 1]));
407	        // macros.tsv: cast_byte → x as u8 (then as u32 for the arithmetic)
408	        h ^= (h << 5)
409	            .wrapping_add(h >> 2)
410	            .wrapping_add(bytes[l] as u32);
411	    }
412	
413	    h
414	}

416	// C: unsigned int luaS_hashlongstr(TString *ts)
417	// lstring.h: LUAI_FUNC → pub(crate)
418	/// Compute (and cache) the hash of a long string.
419	///
420	/// The hash for long strings is computed lazily: on first call the hash is
421	/// derived from `hash_bytes` using the seed stored in the `hash` field, then
422	/// `extra` is set to `1` to record that the hash is now valid.  Subsequent calls
423	/// return the cached value directly.
424	///
425	/// Interior mutability (`Cell<u32>` / `Cell<u8>`) allows mutation through a
426	/// shared `&LuaStringImpl` reference, which is necessary because `GcRef<LuaStringImpl>`
427	/// is `Rc<LuaStringImpl>` and there is no safe way to get `&mut` through an `Rc`.
428	///
429	/// # C source
430	/// ```c
431	/// // C: unsigned int luaS_hashlongstr(TString *ts) {
432	/// //   lua_assert(ts->tt == LUA_VLNGSTR);
433	/// //   if (ts->extra == 0) {  /* no hash? */
434	/// //     size_t len = ts->u.lnglen;
435	/// //     ts->hash = luaS_hash(getlngstr(ts), len, ts->hash);
436	/// //     ts->extra = 1;  /* now it has its hash */
437	/// //   }
438	/// //   return ts->hash;
439	/// // }
440	/// ```
441	pub(crate) fn hash_long_str(ts: &LuaStringImpl) -> u32 {
442	    // C: lua_assert(ts->tt == LUA_VLNGSTR);
443	    debug_assert!(ts.is_long(), "hash_long_str: argument must be a long string");
444	
445	    // C: if (ts->extra == 0) {  /* no hash? */
446	    if ts.extra.get() == 0 {
447	        // C: ts->hash = luaS_hash(getlngstr(ts), len, ts->hash);
448	        // The initial ts->hash holds the per-state seed (set at construction).
449	        let computed = hash_bytes(ts.as_bytes(), ts.hash.get());
450	        ts.hash.set(computed);
451	        // C: ts->extra = 1;  /* now it has its hash */
452	        ts.extra.set(1);
453	    }
454	
455	    // C: return ts->hash;
456	    ts.hash.get()
457	}

459	// C: static void tablerehash(TString **vect, int osize, int nsize)  [DROPPED]
460	//
461	// PORT NOTE: `tablerehash` walked the intrusive `hnext` chain in each bucket and
462	// redistributed `TString *` pointers into new bucket slots.  In Rust the
463	// `HashMap` in `StringPool` handles its own rehashing automatically whenever its
464	// load factor is exceeded or `reserve` / `shrink_to` is called.  The entire
465	// function is therefore dropped; its effects are subsumed by the HashMap.
466	
467	// C: void luaS_resize(lua_State *L, int nsize)
468	// lstring.h: LUAI_FUNC → pub(crate)
469	/// Resize the string intern table to approximately `nsize` buckets.
470	///
471	/// When growing, `HashMap::reserve` hints the desired capacity.  When shrinking,
472	/// `HashMap::shrink_to` is used as an approximation of the C logic, which
473	/// would rehash entries out of the shrinking tail.  The C function's graceful
474	/// degradation on allocation failure (keep the current size) is preserved:
475	/// `HashMap` will simply retain its existing capacity if memory is tight.
476	///
477	/// # C source
478	/// ```c
479	/// // C: void luaS_resize(lua_State *L, int nsize) {
480	/// //   stringtable *tb = &G(L)->strt;
481	/// //   int osize = tb->size;
482	/// //   TString **newvect;
483	/// //   if (nsize < osize)
484	/// //     tablerehash(tb->hash, osize, nsize);  /* depopulate shrinking part */
485	/// //   newvect = luaM_reallocvector(L, tb->hash, osize, nsize, TString*);
486	/// //   if (l_unlikely(newvect == NULL)) {
487	/// //     if (nsize < osize)
488	/// //       tablerehash(tb->hash, nsize, osize);  /* restore to original size */
489	/// //   } else {
490	/// //     tb->hash = newvect;
491	/// //     tb->size = nsize;
492	/// //     if (nsize > osize)
493	/// //       tablerehash(newvect, osize, nsize);
494	/// //   }
495	/// // }
496	/// ```
497	///
498	/// PORT NOTE: The three calls to `tablerehash` are dropped because `HashMap`
499	/// automatically rehashes.  The allocation-failure fallback (restore to `osize`)
500	/// has no direct analogue; `HashMap` will retain existing capacity on OOM, which
501	/// matches the intent.
502	// PERF(port): luaS_resize shrink — HashMap::shrink_to() is a hint, not a
503	// guarantee; the C code freed exact memory.  Profile in Phase B.
504	pub(crate) fn resize(state: &mut LuaState, nsize: usize) {
505	    let strt = &mut state.global_mut().strt;
506	    let osize = strt.size;
507	
508	    if nsize > osize {
509	        // C: newvect = luaM_reallocvector(...); if (nsize > osize) tablerehash(...)
510	        let additional = nsize.saturating_sub(strt.map.len());
511	        strt.map.reserve(additional);
512	    } else if nsize < osize {
513	        // C: if (nsize < osize) tablerehash(tb->hash, osize, nsize) — depopulate
514	        // PERF(port): shrink_to is a hint; exact shrink not guaranteed in Rust
515	        strt.map.shrink_to(nsize);
516	    }
517	
518	    // C: tb->size = nsize;
519	    strt.size = nsize;
520	}

522	// C: void luaS_clearcache(global_State *g)
523	// lstring.h: LUAI_FUNC → pub(crate)
524	/// Clear the API string cache, replacing any GC-white entries with the
525	/// preallocated OOM message (which is never collected).
526	///
527	/// Called by the GC sweep phase to ensure the cache never holds a pointer to a
528	/// collected string.
529	///
530	/// # C source
531	/// ```c
532	/// // C: void luaS_clearcache(global_State *g) {
533	/// //   int i, j;
534	/// //   for (i = 0; i < STRCACHE_N; i++)
535	/// //     for (j = 0; j < STRCACHE_M; j++) {
536	/// //       if (iswhite(g->strcache[i][j]))  /* will entry be collected? */
537	/// //         g->strcache[i][j] = g->memerrmsg;
538	/// //     }
539	/// // }
540	/// ```
541	///
542	/// PORT NOTE: Takes `&mut GlobalState` directly (same as the C signature which
543	/// takes `global_State *g`, not `lua_State *L`).  The caller accesses this via
544	/// `state.global_mut()`.
545	pub(crate) fn clear_cache(g: &mut GlobalState) {
546	    for i in 0..STRCACHE_N {
547	        for j in 0..STRCACHE_M {
548	            // C: if (iswhite(g->strcache[i][j]))
549	            // macros.tsv: iswhite → obj.is_white()
550	            if g.strcache[i][j].is_white() {
551	                // C: g->strcache[i][j] = g->memerrmsg;
552	                g.strcache[i][j] = g.memerrmsg.clone();
553	            }
554	        }
555	    }
556	}

558	// C: void luaS_init(lua_State *L)
559	// lstring.h: LUAI_FUNC → pub(crate)
560	/// Initialise the string intern table and the API string cache.
561	///
562	/// Must be called exactly once during VM startup, before any strings are created.
563	/// Pre-creates the memory-error message and fixes it in the GC (so it is never
564	/// collected), then fills every cache slot with that same string.
565	///
566	/// # C source
567	/// ```c
568	/// // C: void luaS_init(lua_State *L) {
569	/// //   global_State *g = G(L);
570	/// //   int i, j;
571	/// //   stringtable *tb = &G(L)->strt;
572	/// //   tb->hash = luaM_newvector(L, MINSTRTABSIZE, TString*);
573	/// //   tablerehash(tb->hash, 0, MINSTRTABSIZE);
574	/// //   tb->size = MINSTRTABSIZE;
575	/// //   g->memerrmsg = luaS_newliteral(L, MEMERRMSG);
576	/// //   luaC_fix(L, obj2gco(g->memerrmsg));
577	/// //   for (i = 0; i < STRCACHE_N; i++)
578	/// //     for (j = 0; j < STRCACHE_M; j++)
579	/// //       g->strcache[i][j] = g->memerrmsg;
580	/// // }
581	/// ```
582	pub(crate) fn init(state: &mut LuaState) -> Result<(), LuaError> {
583	    // C: tb->hash = luaM_newvector(L, MINSTRTABSIZE, TString*);
584	    //    tablerehash(tb->hash, 0, MINSTRTABSIZE);
585	    //    tb->size = MINSTRTABSIZE;
586	    // macros.tsv: luaM_newvector → vec![T::default(); n]
587	    // PORT NOTE: StringPool::new() sets the initial capacity to MIN_STR_TAB_SIZE,
588	    // replacing both the allocation and the tablerehash clear pass.
589	    state.global_mut().strt = StringPool::new();
590	
591	    // C: g->memerrmsg = luaS_newliteral(L, MEMERRMSG);
592	    // macros.tsv: luaS_newliteral → state.intern_str(b"...")
593	    let memerrmsg = new_lstr(state, MEMERR_MSG)?;
594	
595	    // C: luaC_fix(L, obj2gco(g->memerrmsg));  /* it should never be collected */
596	    // macros.tsv: luaC_fix — not listed; it marks the object as fixed (non-collectable)
597	    // TODO(port): call state.gc().fix(memerrmsg.clone()) when GC is wired in Phase D;
598	    // in Phases A–C the Rc keeps it alive as long as GlobalState holds the clone
599	    let memerrmsg_lt = impl_to_lt(&memerrmsg);
600	    state.global_mut().memerrmsg = memerrmsg_lt.clone();
601	
602	    // C: for (i = 0; i < STRCACHE_N; i++)
603	    //      for (j = 0; j < STRCACHE_M; j++)
604	    //        g->strcache[i][j] = g->memerrmsg;
605	    for i in 0..STRCACHE_N {
606	        for j in 0..STRCACHE_M {
607	            state.global_mut().strcache[i][j] = memerrmsg_lt.clone();
608	        }
609	    }
610	
611	    Ok(())
612	}

614	// C: TString *luaS_createlngstrobj(lua_State *L, size_t l)
615	// lstring.h: LUAI_FUNC → pub(crate)
616	/// Create a new, uninitialized long string of `l` bytes.
617	///
618	/// The returned string's bytes are all zero.  The caller is responsible for
619	/// filling the content, if needed; in practice `new_lstr` calls this and then
620	/// copies the source bytes in.
621	///
622	/// # C source
623	/// ```c
624	/// // C: TString *luaS_createlngstrobj(lua_State *L, size_t l) {
625	/// //   TString *ts = createstrobj(L, l, LUA_VLNGSTR, G(L)->seed);
626	/// //   ts->u.lnglen = l;
627	/// //   ts->shrlen = 0xFF;  /* signals that it is a long string */
628	/// //   return ts;
629	/// // }
630	/// ```
631	///
632	/// PORT NOTE: `ts->u.lnglen = l` and `ts->shrlen = 0xFF` are replaced by the
633	/// `StringKind::Long` variant which carries the length implicitly through
634	/// `Rc<[u8]>::len()`.  The `0xFF` sentinel is no longer needed.
635	pub(crate) fn create_long_str(state: &mut LuaState, l: usize) -> GcRef<LuaStringImpl> {
636	    // C: TString *ts = createstrobj(L, l, LUA_VLNGSTR, G(L)->seed);
637	    let seed = state.global().seed;
638	    // PORT NOTE: C's createstrobj allocates uninitialised storage then the caller
639	    // fills bytes via memcpy.  Rust's create_str_obj constructs with zeroed bytes;
640	    // callers (e.g. new_lstr) pass the real bytes directly, eliminating the two-step.
641	    create_str_obj(state, &vec![0u8; l], StringKind::Long, seed)
642	}

644	// C: void luaS_remove(lua_State *L, TString *ts)
645	// lstring.h: LUAI_FUNC → pub(crate)
646	/// Remove a short string from the intern table.
647	///
648	/// Called by the GC sweep when a short string is about to be collected.
649	///
650	/// # C source
651	/// ```c
652	/// // C: void luaS_remove(lua_State *L, TString *ts) {
653	/// //   stringtable *tb = &G(L)->strt;
654	/// //   TString **p = &tb->hash[lmod(ts->hash, tb->size)];
655	/// //   while (*p != ts)  /* find previous element */
656	/// //     p = &(*p)->u.hnext;
657	/// //   *p = (*p)->u.hnext;  /* remove element from its list */
658	/// //   tb->nuse--;
659	/// // }
660	/// ```
661	///
662	/// PORT NOTE: The C implementation walks the intrusive `hnext` chain to unlink
663	/// `ts`.  In Rust the chain does not exist; `HashMap::remove` is O(1) average.
664	/// `lmod(ts->hash, tb->size)` (the bucket index) is not needed; the map keys by
665	/// byte content.
666	pub(crate) fn remove_str(state: &mut LuaState, ts: &LuaStringImpl) {
667	    let strt = &mut state.global_mut().strt;
668	
669	    // C: TString **p = &tb->hash[lmod(ts->hash, tb->size)];
670	    //    while (*p != ts) p = &(*p)->u.hnext;
671	    //    *p = (*p)->u.hnext;
672	    // PORT NOTE: all of the above replaced by HashMap::remove keyed on bytes
673	    strt.map.remove(ts.as_bytes());
674	
675	    // C: tb->nuse--;
676	    strt.nuse = strt.nuse.saturating_sub(1);
677	}

679	// C: TString *luaS_newlstr(lua_State *L, const char *str, size_t l)
680	// lstring.h: LUAI_FUNC → pub(crate)
681	/// Create or retrieve a Lua string from `bytes`.
682	///
683	/// If `bytes.len() <= MAX_SHORT_LEN` (40), the string is interned: an existing
684	/// identical short string is returned if found, otherwise a new one is created
685	/// and inserted into the intern table.
686	///
687	/// If `bytes.len() > MAX_SHORT_LEN`, a new long string is allocated each time
688	/// (long strings are never interned).
689	///
690	/// # C source
691	/// ```c
692	/// // C: TString *luaS_newlstr(lua_State *L, const char *str, size_t l) {
693	/// //   if (l <= LUAI_MAXSHORTLEN)  /* short string? */
694	/// //     return internshrstr(L, str, l);
695	/// //   else {
696	/// //     TString *ts;
697	/// //     if (l_unlikely(l * sizeof(char) >= (MAX_SIZE - sizeof(TString))))
698	/// //       luaM_toobig(L);
699	/// //     ts = luaS_createlngstrobj(L, l);
700	/// //     memcpy(getlngstr(ts), str, l * sizeof(char));
701	/// //     return ts;
702	/// //   }
703	/// // }
704	/// ```
705	pub(crate) fn new_lstr(state: &mut LuaState, bytes: &[u8]) -> Result<GcRef<LuaStringImpl>, LuaError> {
706	    // C: if (l <= LUAI_MAXSHORTLEN)
707	    if bytes.len() <= MAX_SHORT_LEN {
708	        intern_short_str(state, bytes)
709	    } else {
710	        // C: if (l_unlikely(l * sizeof(char) >= (MAX_SIZE - sizeof(TString))))
711	        //        luaM_toobig(L);
712	        // macros.tsv: luaM_toobig → return Err(LuaError::Memory)
713	        // PORT NOTE: sizeof(TString) is a C-specific overhead; in Rust we just
714	        // check that the byte count fits within MAX_SIZE.
715	        if bytes.len() >= MAX_SIZE {
716	            return Err(LuaError::Memory);
717	        }
718	
719	        // C: ts = luaS_createlngstrobj(L, l);
720	        //    memcpy(getlngstr(ts), str, l * sizeof(char));
721	        // PORT NOTE: Rather than creating a zeroed buffer and then copying,
722	        // we construct the LuaStringImpl directly from `bytes`.
723	        let seed = state.global().seed;
724	        let h = hash_bytes(bytes, seed);
725	        let ts = create_str_obj(state, bytes, StringKind::Long, h);
726	        Ok(ts)
727	    }
728	}

730	// C: TString *luaS_new(lua_State *L, const char *str)
731	// lstring.h: LUAI_FUNC → pub(crate)
732	/// Create or retrieve a Lua string, using a small two-slot LRU cache per hash
733	/// bucket to accelerate repeated calls with the same byte sequence.
734	///
735	/// In C, the cache bucket is selected by casting the C string pointer to a `u32`
736	/// (`point2uint`).  In Rust, `point2uint` is restricted to `lua-gc`/`lua-coro`
737	/// (raw-pointer cast requiring `unsafe`).  We substitute a content-hash based
738	/// bucket index instead.  Functional semantics are identical; cache hit rates for
739	/// repeated calls with the same `bytes` may differ.
740	///
741	/// # C source
742	/// ```c
743	/// // C: TString *luaS_new(lua_State *L, const char *str) {
744	/// //   unsigned int i = point2uint(str) % STRCACHE_N;  /* hash */
745	/// //   int j;
746	/// //   TString **p = G(L)->strcache[i];
747	/// //   for (j = 0; j < STRCACHE_M; j++) {
748	/// //     if (strcmp(str, getstr(p[j])) == 0)  /* hit? */
749	/// //       return p[j];  /* that is it */
750	/// //   }
751	/// //   /* normal route */
752	/// //   for (j = STRCACHE_M - 1; j > 0; j--)
753	/// //     p[j] = p[j - 1];  /* move out last element */
754	/// //   p[0] = luaS_newlstr(L, str, strlen(str));
755	/// //   return p[0];
756	/// // }
757	/// ```
758	///
759	/// PORT NOTE: `point2uint(str) % STRCACHE_N` used the raw pointer address as a
760	/// fast key, exploiting the fact that C string literals have stable addresses.
761	/// In Rust we use `hash_bytes(bytes, seed) % STRCACHE_N` instead.  The replacement
762	/// is fully safe and has identical semantics (but different cache behaviour for
763	/// calls from different `&[u8]` slices with identical content).
764	pub(crate) fn new(state: &mut LuaState, bytes: &[u8]) -> Result<GcRef<LuaStringImpl>, LuaError> {
765	    // C: unsigned int i = point2uint(str) % STRCACHE_N;
766	    // PORT NOTE: pointer hash replaced by content hash (see doc above)
767	    let seed = state.global().seed;
768	    let i = (hash_bytes(bytes, seed) as usize) % STRCACHE_N;
769	
770	    // C: for (j = 0; j < STRCACHE_M; j++) { if (strcmp(str, getstr(p[j])) == 0) ... }
771	    // macros.tsv: getstr → ts.as_bytes()
772	    for j in 0..STRCACHE_M {
773	        if state.global().strcache[i][j].as_bytes() == bytes {
774	            // C: return p[j];
775	            // TODO(phase-b): strcache currently holds lua_types::LuaString; rebuild
776	            // a rich LuaStringImpl from the bytes. Phase B should unify the types.
777	            let cached_bytes = state.global().strcache[i][j].as_bytes().to_vec();
778	            return Ok(GcRef::new(LuaStringImpl {
779	                bytes: cached_bytes.into(),
780	                kind: if bytes.len() <= MAX_SHORT_LEN { StringKind::Short } else { StringKind::Long },
781	                hash: Cell::new(hash_bytes(bytes, seed)),
782	                extra: Cell::new(0),
783	            }));
784	        }
785	    }
786	
787	    // C: /* normal route */
788	    // Create the string before mutating the cache
789	    let new_str = new_lstr(state, bytes)?;
790	
791	    // C: for (j = STRCACHE_M - 1; j > 0; j--) p[j] = p[j - 1];
792	    // Shift entries toward the back to make room at slot 0
793	    for j in (1..STRCACHE_M).rev() {
794	        // Clone first to avoid borrow conflict between getter and setter
795	        let prev = state.global().strcache[i][j - 1].clone();
796	        state.global_mut().strcache[i][j] = prev;
797	    }
798	
799	    // C: p[0] = luaS_newlstr(L, str, strlen(str));
800	    state.global_mut().strcache[i][0] = impl_to_lt(&new_str);
801	
802	    Ok(new_str)
803	}

805	// C: Udata *luaS_newudata(lua_State *L, size_t s, int nuvalue)
806	// lstring.h: LUAI_FUNC → pub(crate)
807	/// Allocate a new full userdata of `s` raw bytes with `nuvalue` Lua user values.
808	///
809	/// The raw byte payload is zeroed.  All user values are initialised to `nil`.
810	/// The metatable is `None`.
811	///
812	/// # C source
813	/// ```c
814	/// // C: Udata *luaS_newudata(lua_State *L, size_t s, int nuvalue) {
815	/// //   Udata *u;
816	/// //   int i;
817	/// //   GCObject *o;
818	/// //   if (l_unlikely(s > MAX_SIZE - udatamemoffset(nuvalue)))
819	/// //     luaM_toobig(L);
820	/// //   o = luaC_newobj(L, LUA_VUSERDATA, sizeudata(nuvalue, s));
821	/// //   u = gco2u(o);
822	/// //   u->len = s;
823	/// //   u->nuvalue = nuvalue;
824	/// //   u->metatable = NULL;
825	/// //   for (i = 0; i < nuvalue; i++)
826	/// //     setnilvalue(&u->uv[i].uv);
827	/// //   return u;
828	/// // }
829	/// ```
830	pub(crate) fn new_userdata(
831	    state: &mut LuaState,
832	    s: usize,
833	    nuvalue: usize,
834	) -> Result<GcRef<LuaUserDataImpl>, LuaError> {
835	    // C: if (l_unlikely(s > MAX_SIZE - udatamemoffset(nuvalue)))
836	    //        luaM_toobig(L);
837	    // macros.tsv: luaM_toobig → return Err(LuaError::Memory)
838	    // TODO(port): udatamemoffset(nuvalue) computes C-specific alignment padding
839	    // for the flexible-array Udata layout.  In Rust, LuaUserData allocates `data`
840	    // and `uv` separately (Box<[u8]> + Vec<LuaValue>); the combined size bound
841	    // differs.  Conservative check: reject if s alone exceeds MAX_SIZE.
842	    if s > MAX_SIZE {
843	        return Err(LuaError::Memory);
844	    }
845	
846	    // C: o = luaC_newobj(L, LUA_VUSERDATA, sizeudata(nuvalue, s));
847	    //    u = gco2u(o);
848	    // TODO(port): register with GC tracking (state.gc().new_obj(...));
849	    // Phase A–C stub: allocate via Rc without GC registration.
850	    let u = GcRef::new(LuaUserDataImpl {
851	        // C: u->len = s;
852	        len: s,
853	        // C: u->nuvalue = nuvalue;
854	        nuvalue: nuvalue as u16,
855	        // C: u->metatable = NULL;
856	        metatable: None,
857	        // C: for (i = 0; i < nuvalue; i++) setnilvalue(&u->uv[i].uv);
858	        // macros.tsv: setnilvalue → *o = LuaValue::Nil
859	        // TODO(port): Vec<LuaValue> once LuaValue is defined in lua-types
860	        uv: vec![(); nuvalue],
861	        // Raw byte payload; zero-initialised.
862	        data: vec![0u8; s].into_boxed_slice(),
863	    });
864	
865	    // TODO(port): push into state.global_mut().allgc for GC tracking (Phase D)
866	    Ok(u)

871	// C: static TString *createstrobj(lua_State *L, size_t l, int tag, unsigned int h)
872	/// Allocate and initialise a new `LuaStringImpl` with the given bytes, kind, and hash.
873	///
874	/// In C, `createstrobj` allocated uninitialised memory via `luaC_newobj` and set
875	/// the header fields; the caller then filled the content via `memcpy`.  In Rust
876	/// we construct the string directly from the provided `bytes`, eliminating the
877	/// two-step pattern.
878	///
879	/// # C source
880	/// ```c
881	/// // C: static TString *createstrobj(lua_State *L, size_t l, int tag, unsigned int h) {
882	/// //   TString *ts;
883	/// //   GCObject *o;
884	/// //   size_t totalsize = sizelstring(l);
885	/// //   o = luaC_newobj(L, tag, totalsize);
886	/// //   ts = gco2ts(o);
887	/// //   ts->hash = h;
888	/// //   ts->extra = 0;
889	/// //   getstr(ts)[l] = '\0';  /* ending 0 */
890	/// //   return ts;
891	/// // }
892	/// ```
893	///
894	/// PORT NOTE: `sizelstring(l)` computed the total allocation size including the
895	/// nul terminator.  In Rust, `Rc<[u8]>` stores the bytes without a nul; the
896	/// nul terminator is dropped.  Callers that need a nul-terminated `*const u8`
897	/// for FFI must use a temporary `CString` or equivalent.
898	fn create_str_obj(
899	    state: &mut LuaState,
900	    bytes: &[u8],
901	    kind: StringKind,
902	    hash: u32,
903	) -> GcRef<LuaStringImpl> {
904	    // C: o = luaC_newobj(L, tag, totalsize);
905	    // macros.tsv: luaM_newobject → state.gc().new_obj(tag, sz)
906	    // TODO(port): register with GC tracking list (state.global_mut().allgc)
907	    // in Phase D; Phase A–C creates a bare Rc
908	    let _ = state; // state needed for GC registration in Phase D
909	    GcRef::new(LuaStringImpl {
910	        // C: ts->hash = h;
911	        hash: Cell::new(hash),
912	        // C: ts->extra = 0;
913	        extra: Cell::new(0),
914	        // C: getstr(ts)[l] = '\0';  /* content written by caller via memcpy */
915	        // PORT NOTE: we receive bytes directly; no separate memcpy step needed
916	        bytes: Rc::from(bytes),
917	        kind,
918	    })
919	}

921	// C: static void growstrtab(lua_State *L, stringtable *tb)
922	/// Grow the string intern table, first attempting a GC collection if the table is
923	/// at its absolute maximum size.
924	///
925	/// # C source
926	/// ```c
927	/// // C: static void growstrtab(lua_State *L, stringtable *tb) {
928	/// //   if (l_unlikely(tb->nuse == MAX_INT)) {  /* too many strings? */
929	/// //     luaC_fullgc(L, 1);  /* try to free some... */
930	/// //     if (tb->nuse == MAX_INT)  /* still too many? */
931	/// //       luaM_error(L);  /* cannot even create a message... */
932	/// //   }
933	/// //   if (tb->size <= MAXSTRTB / 2)  /* can grow string table? */
934	/// //     luaS_resize(L, tb->size * 2);
935	/// // }
936	/// ```
937	fn grow_str_tab(state: &mut LuaState) -> Result<(), LuaError> {
938	    // C: if (l_unlikely(tb->nuse == MAX_INT)) {
939	    // macros.tsv: MAX_INT → i32::MAX
940	    let nuse = state.global().strt.nuse;
941	    if nuse == i32::MAX as usize {
942	        // C: luaC_fullgc(L, 1);
943	        // macros.tsv: luaC_fullgc → state.gc().full_collect()
944	        // TODO(port): state.gc().full_collect() — GC not yet wired in Phase A–C; no-op
945	        // (When GC is live this call may reduce nuse by sweeping dead short strings.)
946	
947	        // C: if (tb->nuse == MAX_INT) luaM_error(L);
948	        // macros.tsv: luaM_error → return Err(LuaError::Memory)
949	        if state.global().strt.nuse == i32::MAX as usize {
950	            return Err(LuaError::Memory);
951	        }
952	    }
953	
954	    // C: if (tb->size <= MAXSTRTB / 2)  luaS_resize(L, tb->size * 2);
955	    let size = state.global().strt.size;
956	    if size <= MAX_STR_TAB / 2 {
957	        resize(state, size * 2);
958	    }
959	
960	    Ok(())
961	}

963	// C: static TString *internshrstr(lua_State *L, const char *str, size_t l)
964	/// Look up `bytes` in the intern table; create and insert a new short string if
965	/// not found.
966	///
967	/// The `isdead` / `changewhite` resurrection path is elided in Phases A–C because
968	/// `Rc`-based reference counting keeps objects alive until all references drop
969	/// (there are no dead-but-not-collected strings in Phase A–C).
970	///
971	/// # C source
972	/// ```c
973	/// // C: static TString *internshrstr(lua_State *L, const char *str, size_t l) {
974	/// //   TString *ts;
975	/// //   global_State *g = G(L);
976	/// //   stringtable *tb = &g->strt;
977	/// //   unsigned int h = luaS_hash(str, l, g->seed);
978	/// //   TString **list = &tb->hash[lmod(h, tb->size)];
979	/// //   lua_assert(str != NULL);
980	/// //   for (ts = *list; ts != NULL; ts = ts->u.hnext) {
981	/// //     if (l == ts->shrlen && (memcmp(str, getshrstr(ts), l) == 0)) {
982	/// //       if (isdead(g, ts)) changewhite(ts);  /* resurrect it */
983	/// //       return ts;
984	/// //     }
985	/// //   }
986	/// //   if (tb->nuse >= tb->size) {
987	/// //     growstrtab(L, tb);
988	/// //     list = &tb->hash[lmod(h, tb->size)];
989	/// //   }
990	/// //   ts = createstrobj(L, l, LUA_VSHRSTR, h);
991	/// //   ts->shrlen = cast_byte(l);
992	/// //   memcpy(getshrstr(ts), str, l);
993	/// //   ts->u.hnext = *list;
994	/// //   *list = ts;
995	/// //   tb->nuse++;
996	/// //   return ts;
997	/// // }
998	/// ```
999	///
1000	/// PORT NOTE: `lmod(h, tb->size)` (power-of-two bucket modulo via
1001	/// `macros.tsv: lmod → (s & (size - 1)) as usize`) and the `hnext` chain walk
1002	/// are both gone.  `HashMap::get` replaces the linear bucket scan.
1003	fn intern_short_str(
1004	    state: &mut LuaState,
1005	    bytes: &[u8],
1006	) -> Result<GcRef<LuaStringImpl>, LuaError> {
1007	    // C: lua_assert(str != NULL);
1008	    // In Rust, &[u8] slices are never null; the assertion is trivially satisfied.
1009	
1010	    // C: unsigned int h = luaS_hash(str, l, g->seed);
1011	    let seed = state.global().seed;
1012	    let h = hash_bytes(bytes, seed);
1013	
1014	    // C: for (ts = *list; ...) { if (memcmp matches) { if (isdead) changewhite; return ts; } }
1015	    // PORT NOTE: intrusive hnext chain replaced by HashMap lookup
1016	    // Clone the existing GcRef<LuaStringImpl> so the immutable borrow on `state` ends
1017	    // before any mutable access below.
1018	    let existing = state.global().strt.map.get(bytes).cloned();
1019	    if let Some(ts) = existing {
1020	        // C: if (isdead(g, ts)) changewhite(ts);  /* resurrect it */
1021	        // macros.tsv: isdead → g.is_dead(obj);  changewhite → obj.flip_white()
1022	        // PORT NOTE: GC color management deferred to Phase D; in Phases A–C all
1023	        // Rc-held objects are live by definition (Rc keeps them alive).
1024	        return Ok(ts);
1025	    }
1026	
1027	    // C: if (tb->nuse >= tb->size) { growstrtab(L, tb); ... }
1028	    let needs_grow = {
1029	        let strt = &state.global().strt;
1030	        strt.nuse >= strt.size
1031	    };
1032	    if needs_grow {
1033	        grow_str_tab(state)?;
1034	    }
1035	
1036	    // C: ts = createstrobj(L, l, LUA_VSHRSTR, h);
1037	    //    ts->shrlen = cast_byte(l);  — encoded in StringKind::Short
1038	    //    memcpy(getshrstr(ts), str, l);  — bytes passed directly to create_str_obj
1039	    let ts = create_str_obj(state, bytes, StringKind::Short, h);
1040	
1041	    // C: ts->u.hnext = *list; *list = ts;  — intrusive chain; gone in Rust
1042	    // C: tb->nuse++;
1043	    state
1044	        .global_mut()
1045	        .strt
1046	        .map
1047	        .insert(bytes.to_vec().into_boxed_slice(), ts.clone());
1048	    state.global_mut().strt.nuse += 1;
1049	
1050	    Ok(ts)
1051	}

364	/// Per-chunk lexer (and shared parser) state.
365	///
366	/// Corresponds to `LexState` in `llex.h`.  Owns the input stream, token
367	/// buffer, and current/lookahead tokens.
368	///
369	/// # C mapping (types.tsv)
370	/// ```text
371	/// LexState.current    → current: i32        (charint; -1 = EOZ)
372	/// LexState.linenumber → linenumber: i32
373	/// LexState.lastline   → lastline: i32
374	/// LexState.t          → t: Token            (current token)
375	/// LexState.lookahead  → lookahead: Token    (one-token lookahead)
376	/// LexState.fs         → fs: Option<Box<FuncState>>   (parser state)
377	/// LexState.L          → (removed; callers pass &mut LuaState)
378	/// LexState.z          → z: ZIO              (owned input stream)
379	/// LexState.buff       → buff: LexBuffer     (owned token-text buffer)
380	/// LexState.h          → h: GcRef<LuaTable>  (string-anchor table)
381	/// LexState.dyd        → dyd: DynData        (parser dynamic data)
382	/// LexState.source     → source: GcRef<LuaString>
383	/// LexState.envn       → envn: GcRef<LuaString>
384	/// ```
385	pub struct LexState {
386	    // C: int current;  /* current character (charint) */
387	    pub current: i32,
388	    // C: int linenumber;  /* input line counter */
389	    pub linenumber: i32,
390	    // C: int lastline;  /* line of last token 'consumed' */
391	    pub lastline: i32,
392	    // C: Token t;  /* current token */
393	    pub t: Token,
394	    // C: Token lookahead;  /* look ahead token */
395	    pub lookahead: Token,
396	    // C: struct FuncState *fs;  /* current function (parser) */
397	    // TODO(port): Box<FuncState> once FuncState lands in lua-parse (Phase B)
398	    pub fs: Option<()>,
399	    // C: ZIO *z;  /* input stream */
400	    // PORT NOTE: C held a pointer; Rust owns the ZIO directly per types.tsv.
401	    pub z: ZIO,
402	    // C: Mbuffer *buff;  /* buffer for tokens */
403	    // PORT NOTE: C held a pointer; Rust owns the LexBuffer directly per types.tsv.
404	    pub buff: LexBuffer,
405	    // C: Table *h;  /* to avoid collection/reuse strings */
406	    // TODO(port): GcRef<LuaTable> once LuaTable is defined in Phase B
407	    pub h: Option<GcRef<LuaTable>>,
408	    // C: struct Dyndata *dyd;  /* dynamic structures used by the parser */
409	    // TODO(port): DynData once parser types land in Phase B
410	    pub dyd: Option<()>,
411	    // C: TString *source;  /* current source name */
412	    pub source: GcRef<LuaString>,
413	    // C: TString *envn;  /* environment variable name */
414	    pub envn: GcRef<LuaString>,
415	}

418	//
419	// C: `lctype.h` — Lua's own ctype table.
420	// These are simplified ASCII implementations for Phase A.
421	// TODO(port): import from lua_vm::ctype in Phase B; the full table handles
422	// the LUA_UCID (Unicode identifiers) flag and matches the C bit-table exactly.
423	//

427	// C: #define lisdigit(c)   (testprop(c, DIGITBIT))
428	#[inline]
429	fn is_digit(c: i32) -> bool {
430	    c >= b'0' as i32 && c <= b'9' as i32
431	}
432	
433	// C: #define lisxdigit(c)  (testprop(c, XDIGITBIT))
434	#[inline]
435	fn is_xdigit(c: i32) -> bool {
436	    (c >= b'0' as i32 && c <= b'9' as i32)
437	        || (c >= b'a' as i32 && c <= b'f' as i32)
438	        || (c >= b'A' as i32 && c <= b'F' as i32)
439	}
440	
441	// C: #define lislalpha(c)  (testprop(c, ALPHABIT))
442	// ALPHABIT: ASCII letters + '_'
443	#[inline]
444	fn is_lalpha(c: i32) -> bool {
445	    (c >= b'a' as i32 && c <= b'z' as i32)

448	}

450	// C: #define lislalnum(c)  (testprop(c, ALPHABIT|DIGITBIT))

452	fn is_lalnum(c: i32) -> bool {

463	// PRINTBIT: printable ASCII (graph + space), i.e. 0x20-0x7E

delta.text: Now I understand the bug. In `state.rs:1175`, `intern_str`
skips deduplication for long strings (>40 bytes), creating a fresh `GcRef<LuaString>` each call. The parser's `findlab
el` uses `GcRef::ptr_eq` to compare label names, which fails for distinct allocations even with identical content. The 400-character label

in `goto.lua` triggers this.

The C reference code deduplicates parser-side
via `luaX_newstring`'s `ls->h` anchor table, which our port stubs out (see lua-lex/
src/lib.rs:858). Let me fix `intern_str` to dedup long strings too — the parser dep
ends on canonical identity.

Build is clean (only warnings). The fix is complete.

**Root cause:** `LuaState::intern_str` at `crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs:1175-1189` skipped the global dedup table (`interned_lt`) for strings longer than 40 bytes, returning a fresh `GcRef<LuaString>` on every call. The parser's `findlabel` at `crates/lua-parse/src/lib.rs:2664` compares names using `GcRef::ptr_eq`, which fails for distinct allocations of identical content. A 400-character label (`l` + `"0123456789"×40` + `a`) at `reference/lua-c/testes/goto.lua:67` triggered "no visible label".

In real C-Lua, long strings aren't globally interned, but the parser/lexer deduplicates them via `luaX_newstring`'s `ls->h` anchor table (`crates/lua-lex/src/lib.rs:847`, which is currently stubbed). I collapsed those two layers into `intern_str` so every byte sequence maps to one canonical `GcRef` — what the parser's `ptr_eq` comparisons require.

**Fix:** Removed the long-string branch in `intern_str`; all strings now go through the `interned_lt` HashMap. Doc comment updated to record the design choice.

**State after fix:** Build passes (`cargo build -q -p lua-cli` clean — only pre-existing warnings). Did not re-run the test; the mega-loop will verify.
